NKJV.1: Praise the LORD! Oh, give thanks to the LORD, for He is good! For His mercy endures forever.
NKJV.2: Who can utter the mighty acts of the LORD? Who can declare all His praise?
NKJV.3: Blessed are those who keep justice, And he who does righteousness at all times!
NKJV.4: Remember me, O LORD, with the favor You have toward Your people. Oh, visit me with Your salvation,
NKJV.5: That I may see the benefit of Your chosen ones, That I may rejoice in the gladness of Your nation, That I may glory with Your inheritance.
NKJV.6: We have sinned with our fathers, We have committed iniquity, We have done wickedly.
NKJV.7: Our fathers in Egypt did not understand Your wonders; They did not remember the multitude of Your mercies,  But rebelled by the sea—the Red Sea.
NKJV.8: Nevertheless He saved them for His name’s sake,  That He might make His mighty power known.
NKJV.9: He rebuked the Red Sea also, and it dried up; So He led them through the depths, As through the wilderness.
NKJV.10: He saved them from the hand of him who hated them, And redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
NKJV.11: The waters covered their enemies; There was not one of them left.
NKJV.12: Then they believed His words; They sang His praise.
NKJV.13: They soon forgot His works; They did not wait for His counsel,
NKJV.14: But lusted exceedingly in the wilderness, And tested God in the desert.
NKJV.15: And He gave them their request, But sent leanness into their soul.
NKJV.16: When they envied Moses in the camp, And Aaron the saint of the LORD,
NKJV.17: The earth opened up and swallowed Dathan, And covered the faction of Abiram.
NKJV.18: A fire was kindled in their company; The flame burned up the wicked.
NKJV.19: They made a calf in Horeb, And worshiped the molded image.
NKJV.20: Thus they changed their glory Into the image of an ox that eats grass.
NKJV.21: They forgot God their Savior, Who had done great things in Egypt,
NKJV.22: Wondrous works in the land of Ham, Awesome things by the Red Sea.
NKJV.23: Therefore He said that He would destroy them, Had not Moses His chosen one stood before Him in the breach, To turn away His wrath, lest He destroy them.
NKJV.24: Then they despised the pleasant land; They did not believe His word,
NKJV.25: But complained in their tents, And did not heed the voice of the LORD.
NKJV.26: Therefore He raised His hand in an oath against them, To overthrow them in the wilderness,
NKJV.27: To overthrow their descendants among the nations, And to scatter them in the lands.
NKJV.28: They joined themselves also to Baal of Peor, And ate sacrifices made to the dead.
NKJV.29: Thus they provoked Him to anger with their deeds, And the plague broke out among them.
NKJV.30: Then Phinehas stood up and intervened, And the plague was stopped.
NKJV.31: And that was accounted to him for righteousness To all generations forevermore.
NKJV.32: They angered Him also at the waters of strife, So that it went ill with Moses on account of them;
NKJV.33: Because they rebelled against His Spirit, So that he spoke rashly with his lips.
NKJV.34: They did not destroy the peoples,  Concerning whom the LORD had commanded them,
NKJV.35: But they mingled with the Gentiles And learned their works;
NKJV.36: They served their idols,  Which became a snare to them.
NKJV.37: They even sacrificed their sons And their daughters to demons,
NKJV.38: And shed innocent blood, The blood of their sons and daughters, Whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan; And the land was polluted with blood.
NKJV.39: Thus they were defiled by their own works, And played the harlot by their own deeds.
NKJV.40: Therefore the wrath of the LORD was kindled against His people, So that He abhorred His own inheritance.
NKJV.41: And He gave them into the hand of the Gentiles, And those who hated them ruled over them.
NKJV.42: Their enemies also oppressed them, And they were brought into subjection under their hand.
NKJV.43: Many times He delivered them; But they rebelled in their counsel, And were brought low for their iniquity.
NKJV.44: Nevertheless He regarded their affliction, When He heard their cry;
NKJV.45: And for their sake He remembered His covenant, And relented according to the multitude of His mercies.
NKJV.46: He also made them to be pitied By all those who carried them away captive.
NKJV.47: Save us, O LORD our God, And gather us from among the Gentiles, To give thanks to Your holy name, To triumph in Your praise.
NKJV.48: Blessed be the LORD God of Israel From everlasting to everlasting! And let all the people say, “Amen!” Praise the LORD!

ESV.1: Praise the LORD! Oh give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever!
ESV.2: Who can utter the mighty deeds of the LORD, or declare all his praise?
ESV.3: Blessed are they who observe justice, who do righteousness at all times!
ESV.4: Remember me, O LORD, when you show favor to your people; help me when you save them,
ESV.5: that I may look upon the prosperity of your chosen ones, that I may rejoice in the gladness of your nation, that I may glory with your inheritance.
ESV.6: Both we and our fathers have sinned; we have committed iniquity; we have done wickedness.
ESV.7: Our fathers, when they were in Egypt, did not consider your wondrous works; they did not remember the abundance of your steadfast love, but rebelled by the sea, at the Red Sea.
ESV.8: Yet he saved them for his name's sake, that he might make known his mighty power.
ESV.9: He rebuked the Red Sea, and it became dry, and he led them through the deep as through a desert.
ESV.10: So he saved them from the hand of the foe and redeemed them from the power of the enemy.
ESV.11: And the waters covered their adversaries; not one of them was left.
ESV.12: Then they believed his words; they sang his praise.
ESV.13: But they soon forgot his works; they did not wait for his counsel.
ESV.14: But they had a wanton craving in the wilderness, and put God to the test in the desert;
ESV.15: he gave them what they asked, but sent a wasting disease among them.
ESV.16: When men in the camp were jealous of Moses and Aaron, the holy one of the LORD,
ESV.17: the earth opened and swallowed up Dathan, and covered the company of Abiram.
ESV.18: Fire also broke out in their company; the flame burned up the wicked.
ESV.19: They made a calf in Horeb and worshiped a metal image.
ESV.20: They exchanged the glory of God  for the image of an ox that eats grass.
ESV.21: They forgot God, their Savior, who had done great things in Egypt,
ESV.22: wondrous works in the land of Ham, and awesome deeds by the Red Sea.
ESV.23: Therefore he said he would destroy them — had not Moses, his chosen one, stood in the breach before him, to turn away his wrath from destroying them.
ESV.24: Then they despised the pleasant land, having no faith in his promise.
ESV.25: They murmured in their tents, and did not obey the voice of the LORD.
ESV.26: Therefore he raised his hand and swore to them that he would make them fall in the wilderness,
ESV.27: and would make their offspring fall among the nations, scattering them among the lands.
ESV.28: Then they yoked themselves to the Baal of Peor, and ate sacrifices offered to the dead;
ESV.29: they provoked the LORD to anger with their deeds, and a plague broke out among them.
ESV.30: Then Phinehas stood up and intervened, and the plague was stayed.
ESV.31: And that was counted to him as righteousness from generation to generation forever.
ESV.32: They angered him at the waters of Meribah, and it went ill with Moses on their account,
ESV.33: for they made his spirit bitter,  and he spoke rashly with his lips.
ESV.34: They did not destroy the peoples, as the LORD commanded them,
ESV.35: but they mixed with the nations and learned to do as they did.
ESV.36: They served their idols, which became a snare to them.
ESV.37: They sacrificed their sons and their daughters to the demons;
ESV.38: they poured out innocent blood, the blood of their sons and daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan, and the land was polluted with blood.
ESV.39: Thus they became unclean by their acts, and played the whore in their deeds.
ESV.40: Then the anger of the LORD was kindled against his people, and he abhorred his heritage;
ESV.41: he gave them into the hand of the nations, so that those who hated them ruled over them.
ESV.42: Their enemies oppressed them, and they were brought into subjection under their power.
ESV.43: Many times he delivered them, but they were rebellious in their purposes and were brought low through their iniquity.
ESV.44: Nevertheless, he looked upon their distress, when he heard their cry.
ESV.45: For their sake he remembered his covenant, and relented according to the abundance of his steadfast love.
ESV.46: He caused them to be pitied by all those who held them captive.
ESV.47: Save us, O LORD our God, and gather us from among the nations, that we may give thanks to your holy name and glory in your praise.
ESV.48: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting! And let all the people say, “Amen!” Praise the LORD!

NASB.1: Praise the LORD! Oh give thanks to the LORD, for He is good; For His lovingkindness is everlasting.
NASB.2: Who can speak of the mighty deeds of the LORD, Or can show forth all His praise?
NASB.3: How blessed are those who keep justice, Who practice righteousness at all times!
NASB.4: Remember me, O LORD, in [Your] favor toward Your people; Visit me with Your salvation,
NASB.5: That I may see the prosperity of Your chosen ones, That I may rejoice in the gladness of Your nation, That I may glory with Your inheritance.
NASB.6: We have sinned like our fathers, We have committed iniquity, we have behaved wickedly.
NASB.7: Our fathers in Egypt did not understand Your wonders; They did not remember Your abundant kindnesses, But rebelled by the sea, at the Red Sea.
NASB.8: Nevertheless He saved them for the sake of His name, That He might make His power known.
NASB.9: Thus He rebuked the Red Sea and it dried up, And He led them through the deeps, as through the wilderness.
NASB.10: So He saved them from the hand of the one who hated [them], And redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
NASB.11: The waters covered their adversaries; Not one of them was left.
NASB.12: Then they believed His words; They sang His praise.
NASB.13: They quickly forgot His works; They did not wait for His counsel,
NASB.14: But craved intensely in the wilderness, And tempted God in the desert.
NASB.15: So He gave them their request, But sent a wasting disease among them.
NASB.16: When they became envious of Moses in the camp, And of Aaron, the holy one of the LORD,
NASB.17: The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan, And engulfed the company of Abiram.
NASB.18: And a fire blazed up in their company; The flame consumed the wicked.
NASB.19: They made a calf in Horeb And worshiped a molten image.
NASB.20: Thus they exchanged their glory For the image of an ox that eats grass.
NASB.21: They forgot God their Savior, Who had done great things in Egypt,
NASB.22: Wonders in the land of Ham [And] awesome things by the Red Sea.
NASB.23: Therefore He said that He would destroy them, Had not Moses His chosen one stood in the breach before Him, To turn away His wrath from destroying [them].
NASB.24: Then they despised the pleasant land; They did not believe in His word,
NASB.25: But grumbled in their tents; They did not listen to the voice of the LORD.
NASB.26: Therefore He swore to them That He would cast them down in the wilderness,
NASB.27: And that He would cast their seed among the nations And scatter them in the lands.
NASB.28: They joined themselves also to Baal-peor, And ate sacrifices offered to the dead.
NASB.29: Thus they provoked [Him] to anger with their deeds, And the plague broke out among them.
NASB.30: Then Phinehas stood up and interposed, And so the plague was stayed.
NASB.31: And it was reckoned to him for righteousness, To all generations forever.
NASB.32: They also provoked [Him] to wrath at the waters of Meribah, So that it went hard with Moses on their account;
NASB.33: Because they were rebellious against His Spirit, He spoke rashly with his lips.
NASB.34: They did not destroy the peoples, As the LORD commanded them,
NASB.35: But they mingled with the nations And learned their practices,
NASB.36: And served their idols, Which became a snare to them.
NASB.37: They even sacrificed their sons and their daughters to the demons,
NASB.38: And shed innocent blood, The blood of their sons and their daughters, Whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan; And the land was polluted with the blood.
NASB.39: Thus they became unclean in their practices, And played the harlot in their deeds.
NASB.40: Therefore the anger of the LORD was kindled against His people And He abhorred His inheritance.
NASB.41: Then He gave them into the hand of the nations, And those who hated them ruled over them.
NASB.42: Their enemies also oppressed them, And they were subdued under their power.
NASB.43: Many times He would deliver them; They, however, were rebellious in their counsel, And [so] sank down in their iniquity.
NASB.44: Nevertheless He looked upon their distress When He heard their cry;
NASB.45: And He remembered His covenant for their sake, And relented according to the greatness of His lovingkindness.
NASB.46: He also made them [objects] of compassion In the presence of all their captors.
NASB.47: Save us, O LORD our God, And gather us from among the nations, To give thanks to Your holy name And glory in Your praise.
NASB.48: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, From everlasting even to everlasting. And let all the people say, 'Amen.' Praise the LORD!

NLT.1:
Praise the LORD!
Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good!
His faithful love endures forever.
NLT.2:
Who can list the glorious miracles of the LORD?
Who can ever praise him enough?
NLT.3:
There is joy for those who deal justly with others
and always do what is right.
NLT.4:
Remember me, LORD, when you show favor to your people;
come near and rescue me.
NLT.5:
Let me share in the prosperity of your chosen ones.
Let me rejoice in the joy of your people;
let me praise you with those who are your heritage.
NLT.6:
Like our ancestors, we have sinned.
We have done wrong! We have acted wickedly!
NLT.7:
Our ancestors in Egypt
were not impressed by the LORD’s miraculous deeds.
They soon forgot his many acts of kindness to them.
Instead, they rebelled against him at the Red Sea.
NLT.8:
Even so, he saved them —
to defend the honor of his name
and to demonstrate his mighty power.
NLT.9:
He commanded the Red Sea to dry up.
He led Israel across the sea as if it were a desert.
NLT.10:
So he rescued them from their enemies
and redeemed them from their foes.
NLT.11:
Then the water returned and covered their enemies;
not one of them survived.
NLT.12:
Then his people believed his promises.
Then they sang his praise.
NLT.13:
Yet how quickly they forgot what he had done!
They wouldn’t wait for his counsel!
NLT.14:
In the wilderness their desires ran wild,
testing God’s patience in that dry wasteland.
NLT.15:
So he gave them what they asked for,
but he sent a plague along with it.
NLT.16:
The people in the camp were jealous of Moses
and envious of Aaron, the LORD’s holy priest.
NLT.17:
Because of this, the earth opened up;
it swallowed Dathan
and buried Abiram and the other rebels.
NLT.18:
Fire fell upon their followers;
a flame consumed the wicked.
NLT.19:
The people made a calf at Mount Sinai ;
they bowed before an image made of gold.
NLT.20:
They traded their glorious God
for a statue of a grass-eating bull.
NLT.21:
They forgot God, their savior,
who had done such great things in Egypt —
NLT.22:
such wonderful things in the land of Ham,
such awesome deeds at the Red Sea.
NLT.23:
So he declared he would destroy them.
But Moses, his chosen one, stepped between the LORD and the people.
He begged him to turn from his anger and not destroy them.
NLT.24:
The people refused to enter the pleasant land,
for they wouldn’t believe his promise to care for them.
NLT.25:
Instead, they grumbled in their tents
and refused to obey the LORD.
NLT.26:
Therefore, he solemnly swore
that he would kill them in the wilderness,
NLT.27:
that he would scatter their descendants among the nations,
exiling them to distant lands.
NLT.28:
Then our ancestors joined in the worship of Baal at Peor;
they even ate sacrifices offered to the dead!
NLT.29:
They angered the LORD with all these things,
so a plague broke out among them.
NLT.30:
But Phinehas had the courage to intervene,
and the plague was stopped.
NLT.31:
So he has been regarded as a righteous man
ever since that time.
NLT.32:
At Meribah, too, they angered the LORD,
causing Moses serious trouble.
NLT.33:
They made Moses angry,
and he spoke foolishly.
NLT.34:
Israel failed to destroy the nations in the land,
as the LORD had commanded them.
NLT.35:
Instead, they mingled among the pagans
and adopted their evil customs.
NLT.36:
They worshiped their idols,
which led to their downfall.
NLT.37:
They even sacrificed their sons
and their daughters to the demons.
NLT.38:
They shed innocent blood,
the blood of their sons and daughters.
By sacrificing them to the idols of Canaan,
they polluted the land with murder.
NLT.39:
They defiled themselves by their evil deeds,
and their love of idols was adultery in the LORD’s sight.
NLT.40:
That is why the LORD’s anger burned against his people,
and he abhorred his own special possession.
NLT.41:
He handed them over to pagan nations,
and they were ruled by those who hated them.
NLT.42:
Their enemies crushed them
and brought them under their cruel power.
NLT.43:
Again and again he rescued them,
but they chose to rebel against him,
and they were finally destroyed by their sin.
NLT.44:
Even so, he pitied them in their distress
and listened to their cries.
NLT.45:
He remembered his covenant with them
and relented because of his unfailing love.
NLT.46:
He even caused their captors
to treat them with kindness.
NLT.47:
Save us, O LORD our God!
Gather us back from among the nations,
so we can thank your holy name
and rejoice and praise you.
NLT.48:
Praise the LORD, the God of Israel,
who lives from everlasting to everlasting!
Let all the people say, “Amen!”
Praise the LORD!

WEB.1: Praise Yahweh! Give thanks to Yahweh, for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.
WEB.2: Who can utter the mighty acts of Yahweh, or fully declare all his praise?
WEB.3: Blessed are those who keep justice. Blessed is one who does what is right at all times.
WEB.4: Remember me, Yahweh, with the favor that you show to your people. Visit me with your salvation,
WEB.5: that I may see the prosperity of your chosen, that I may rejoice in the gladness of your nation, that I may glory with your inheritance.
WEB.6: We have sinned with our fathers. We have committed iniquity. We have done wickedly.
WEB.7: Our fathers didn’t understand your wonders in Egypt. They didn’t remember the multitude of your loving kindnesses, but were rebellious at the sea, even at the Red Sea.
WEB.8: Nevertheless he saved them for his name’s sake, that he might make his mighty power known.
WEB.9: He rebuked the Red Sea also, and it was dried up; so he led them through the depths, as through a desert.
WEB.10: He saved them from the hand of him who hated them, and redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
WEB.11: The waters covered their adversaries. There was not one of them left.
WEB.12: Then they believed his words. They sang his praise.
WEB.13: They soon forgot his works. They didn’t wait for his counsel,
WEB.14: but gave in to craving in the desert, and tested God in the wasteland.
WEB.15: He gave them their request, but sent leanness into their soul.
WEB.16: They envied Moses also in the camp, and Aaron, Yahweh’s saint.
WEB.17: The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan, and covered the company of Abiram.
WEB.18: A fire was kindled in their company. The flame burned up the wicked.
WEB.19: They made a calf in Horeb, and worshiped a molten image.
WEB.20: Thus they exchanged their glory for an image of a bull that eats grass.
WEB.21: They forgot God, their Savior, who had done great things in Egypt,
WEB.22: Wondrous works in the land of Ham, and awesome things by the Red Sea.
WEB.23: Therefore he said that he would destroy them, had Moses, his chosen, not stood before him in the breach, to turn away his wrath, so that he wouldn’t destroy them.
WEB.24: Yes, they despised the pleasant land. They didn’t believe his word,
WEB.25: but murmured in their tents, and didn’t listen to Yahweh’s voice.
WEB.26: Therefore he swore to them that he would overthrow them in the wilderness,
WEB.27: that he would overthrow their offspring  among the nations, and scatter them in the lands.
WEB.28: They joined themselves also to Baal Peor, and ate the sacrifices of the dead.
WEB.29: Thus they provoked him to anger with their deeds. The plague broke in on them.
WEB.30: Then Phinehas stood up, and executed judgment, so the plague was stopped.
WEB.31: That was credited to him for righteousness, for all generations to come.
WEB.32: They angered him also at the waters of Meribah, so that Moses was troubled for their sakes;
WEB.33: because they were rebellious against his spirit, he spoke rashly with his lips.
WEB.34: They didn’t destroy the peoples, as Yahweh commanded them,
WEB.35: but mixed themselves with the nations, and learned their works.
WEB.36: They served their idols, which became a snare to them.
WEB.37: Yes, they sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons.
WEB.38: They shed innocent blood, even the blood of their sons and of their daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan. The land was polluted with blood.
WEB.39: Thus were they defiled with their works, and prostituted themselves in their deeds.
WEB.40: Therefore Yahweh burned with anger against his people. He abhorred his inheritance.
WEB.41: He gave them into the hand of the nations. Those who hated them ruled over them.
WEB.42: Their enemies also oppressed them. They were brought into subjection under their hand.
WEB.43: He rescued them many times, but they were rebellious in their counsel, and were brought low in their iniquity.
WEB.44: Nevertheless he regarded their distress, when he heard their cry.
WEB.45: He remembered for them his covenant, and repented according to the multitude of his loving kindnesses.
WEB.46: He made them also to be pitied by all those who carried them captive.
WEB.47: Save us, Yahweh, our God, gather us from among the nations, to give thanks to your holy name, to triumph in your praise!
WEB.48: Blessed be Yahweh, the God of Israel, from everlasting even to everlasting! Let all the people say, “Amen.” Praise Yah!

CSB17.1:
Hallelujah!
Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good;
his faithful love endures forever. ⓕ
CSB17.2:
Who can declare the LORD’s mighty acts
or proclaim all the praise due him? ⓖ
CSB17.3:
How happy are those who uphold justice,
who practice righteousness at all times. ⓗ
CSB17.4:
Remember me, LORD,
when you show favor to your people. ⓘ
Come to me with your salvation
CSB17.5:
so that I may enjoy the prosperity
of your chosen ones,
rejoice in the joy of your nation,
and boast about your heritage. ⓙ
CSB17.6:
Both we and our fathers have sinned;
we have done wrong and have acted wickedly. ⓚ
CSB17.7:
Our fathers in Egypt did not grasp
the significance of your wondrous works
or remember your many acts of faithful love;
instead, they rebelled by the sea — the Red Sea. ⓛ
CSB17.8:
Yet he saved them for his name’s sake,
to make his power known. ⓜ
CSB17.9:
He rebuked the Red Sea, and it dried up;
he led them through the depths as through a desert. ⓝ
CSB17.10:
He saved them from the power of the adversary;
he redeemed them from the power of the enemy. ⓐ
CSB17.11:
Water covered their foes;
not one of them remained. ⓑ
CSB17.12:
Then they believed his promises
and sang his praise. ⓒ
CSB17.13:
They soon forgot his works
and would not wait for his counsel. ⓓ
CSB17.14:
They were seized with craving in the wilderness
and tested God in the desert. ⓔ
CSB17.15:
He gave them what they asked for,
but sent a wasting disease among them. ⓕ
CSB17.16:
In the camp they were envious of Moses
and of Aaron, the LORD’s holy one. ⓖ
CSB17.17:
The earth opened up and swallowed Dathan;
it covered the assembly of Abiram. ⓗ
CSB17.18:
Fire blazed throughout their assembly;
flames consumed the wicked. ⓘ
CSB17.19:
At Horeb they made a calf
and worshiped the cast metal image. ⓙ
CSB17.20:
They exchanged their glory [1] [2]
for the image of a grass-eating ox. ⓚ
CSB17.21:
They forgot God their Savior,
who did great things in Egypt, ⓛ
CSB17.22:
wondrous works in the land of Ham, [3]
awe-inspiring acts at the Red Sea. ⓜ
CSB17.23:
So he said he would have destroyed them —
if Moses his chosen one
had not stood before him in the breach
to turn his wrath away from destroying them. ⓝ
CSB17.24:
They despised the pleasant land
and did not believe his promise. ⓐ
CSB17.25:
They grumbled in their tents
and did not listen to the LORD. ⓑ
CSB17.26:
So he raised his hand against them with an oath
that he would make them fall in the desert ⓒ
CSB17.27:
and would disperse their descendants [4]
among the nations,
scattering them throughout the lands. ⓓ
CSB17.28:
They aligned themselves with Baal of Peor
and ate sacrifices offered to lifeless gods. [5] ⓔ
CSB17.29:
They angered the LORD with their deeds,
and a plague broke out against them. ⓕ
CSB17.30:
But Phinehas stood up and intervened,
and the plague was stopped. ⓖ
CSB17.31:
It was credited to him as righteousness
throughout all generations to come. ⓗ
CSB17.32:
They angered the LORD at the Waters of Meribah,
and Moses suffered [6] because of them; ⓘ
CSB17.33:
for they embittered his spirit, [7]
and he spoke rashly with his lips. ⓙ
CSB17.34:
They did not destroy the peoples
as the LORD had commanded them ⓚ
CSB17.35:
but mingled with the nations
and adopted their ways. ⓛ
CSB17.36:
They served their idols,
which became a snare to them. ⓜ
CSB17.37: They sacrificed their sons and daughters to demons. ⓝ
CSB17.38:
They shed innocent blood —
the blood of their sons and daughters
whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan;
so the land became polluted with blood. ⓐ
CSB17.39:
They defiled themselves by their actions
and prostituted themselves by their deeds. ⓑ
CSB17.40:
Therefore the LORD’s anger burned against his people,
and he abhorred his own inheritance. ⓒ
CSB17.41:
He handed them over to the nations;
those who hated them ruled over them. ⓓ
CSB17.42:
Their enemies oppressed them,
and they were subdued under their power. ⓔ
CSB17.43:
He rescued them many times,
but they continued to rebel deliberately
and were beaten down by their iniquity. ⓕ
CSB17.44:
When he heard their cry,
he took note of their distress, ⓖ
CSB17.45:
remembered his covenant with them,
and relented according to the abundance
of his faithful love. ⓗ
CSB17.46:
He caused them to be pitied
before all their captors. ⓘ
CSB17.47:
Save us, LORD our God,
and gather us from the nations,
so that we may give thanks to your holy name
and rejoice in your praise. ⓙ
CSB17.48:
Blessed be the LORD God of Israel,
from everlasting to everlasting. ⓚ
Let all the people say, “Amen!”
Hallelujah! ⓛ

AMP.1:
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)
Oh give thanks to the LORD, for He is good;
For His mercy and lovingkindness endure forever!
AMP.2:
Who can put into words the mighty deeds of the LORD?
Or who can proclaim all His praise [that is due Him]?
AMP.3:
Blessed are those who observe justice [by honoring God's precepts],
Who practice righteousness at all times.
AMP.4:
Remember me, O LORD, when You favor Your people.
Visit me with Your salvation [when You rescue them],
AMP.5:
That I may see the prosperity of Your chosen ones,
That I may rejoice in the gladness of Your nation,
That I may glory with Your inheritance.
AMP.6:
We have sinned like our fathers;
We have committed iniquity, we have behaved wickedly.
AMP.7:
Our fathers in Egypt did not understand nor appreciate Your miracles;
They did not remember the abundance of Your mercies nor imprint Your lovingkindnesses on their hearts,
But they were rebellious at the sea, at the Red Sea.
AMP.8:
Nevertheless He saved them for His name's sake,
That He might make His [supreme] power known.
AMP.9:
He rebuked the Red Sea, and it dried up;
And He led them through the depths as through a pasture.
AMP.10:
So He saved them from the hand of the one that hated them,
And redeemed them from the hand of the [Egyptian] enemy.
AMP.11:
And the waters covered their adversaries;
Not one of them was left.
AMP.12:
Then Israel believed in [the validity of] His words;
They sang His praise.
AMP.13:
But they quickly forgot His works;
They did not [patiently] wait for His counsel and purpose [to be revealed regarding them],
AMP.14:
But lusted intensely in the wilderness
And tempted God [with their insistent desires] in the desert.
AMP.15:
So He gave them their request,
But sent a wasting disease among them.
AMP.16:
They envied Moses in the camp,
And Aaron [the high priest], the holy one of the LORD,
AMP.17:
Therefore the earth opened and swallowed Dathan,
And engulfed the company of Abiram.
AMP.18:
And a fire broke out in their company;
The flame consumed the wicked.
AMP.19:
They made a calf in Horeb (Sinai)
And worshiped a cast image.
AMP.20:
Thus they exchanged [the true God who was] their glory
For the image of an ox that eats grass.
AMP.21:
They forgot God their Savior,
Who had done such great things in Egypt,
AMP.22:
Wonders in the land of Ham,
Awesome things at the Red Sea.
AMP.23:
Therefore He said He would destroy them,
[And He would have done so] had not Moses, His chosen one, stepped into the gap before Him,
To turn away His wrath from destroying them.
AMP.24:
Then they despised the pleasant land [of Canaan];
They did not believe in His word nor rely on it,
AMP.25:
But they sulked and complained in their tents;
They did not listen to the voice of the LORD.
AMP.26:
Therefore He lifted up His hand [swearing] to them,
That He would cause them to fall in the wilderness,
AMP.27:
And that He would cast out their descendants among the nations
And scatter them in the lands [of the earth].
AMP.28:
They joined themselves also to [the idol] Baal of Peor,
And ate sacrifices offered to the dead.
AMP.29:
Thus they provoked Him to anger with their practices,
And a plague broke out among them.
AMP.30:
Then Phinehas [the priest] stood up and interceded,
And so the plague was halted.
AMP.31:
And that was credited to him for righteousness,
To all generations forever.
AMP.32:
They provoked Him to anger at the waters of Meribah,
So that it went hard with Moses on their account;
AMP.33:
Because they were rebellious against His Spirit,
Moses spoke recklessly with his lips.
AMP.34:
They did not destroy the [pagan] peoples [in Canaan],
As the LORD commanded them,
AMP.35:
But they mingled with the [idolatrous] nations
And learned their ways,
AMP.36:
And served their idols,
Which became a [dreadful] snare to them.
AMP.37: They even sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons
AMP.38:
And shed innocent blood,
Even the blood of their sons and of their daughters,
Whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan;
And the land was polluted with their blood.
AMP.39:
In this way they became unclean in their practices;
They played the prostitute in their own deeds [by giving their worship, which belongs to God alone, to other "gods"].
AMP.40:
Therefore the anger of the LORD was kindled against His people
And He detested His own inheritance.
AMP.41:
He gave them into the hands of the nations,
And those who hated them ruled over them.
AMP.42:
Their enemies also oppressed them,
And they were subdued under the [powerful] hand of their enemies.
AMP.43:
Many times He rescued them;
But they were rebellious in their counsel,
And sank down in their wickedness.
AMP.44:
Nevertheless He looked [sympathetically] at their distress
When He heard their cry;
AMP.45:
And He remembered His covenant for their sake,
And relented [rescinding their sentence] according to the greatness of His lovingkindness [when they cried out to Him],
AMP.46:
He also made them objects of compassion
Among those who had carried them away captive.
AMP.47:
Save us, O LORD our God,
And gather us from among the nations,
That we may give thanks to Your holy name
And glory in praising You.
AMP.48:
Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel,
From everlasting even to everlasting.
And let all the people say, "Amen."
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)

MSG.1:
Hallelujah!
Thank God! And why?
Because he's good, because his love lasts.
MSG.2:
But who on earth can do it—
declaim God's mighty acts, broadcast all his praises?
MSG.3:
You're one happy man when you do what's right,
one happy woman when you form the habit of justice.
MSG.4:
Remember me, God, when you enjoy your people;
include me when you save them;
MSG.5:
I want to see your chosen succeed,
celebrate with your celebrating nation,
join the Hallelujahs of your pride and joy!
MSG.6:
We've sinned a lot, both we and our parents;
We've fallen short, hurt a lot of people.
MSG.7:
After our parents left Egypt,
they took your wonders for granted,
forgot your great and wonderful love.
They were barely beyond the Red Sea
when they defied the High God
MSG.8:
—the very place he saved them!
—the place he revealed his amazing power!
MSG.9:
He rebuked the Red Sea so that it dried up on the spot
—he paraded them right through!
—no one so much as got wet feet!
MSG.10:
He saved them from a life of oppression,
pried them loose from the grip of the enemy.
MSG.11:
Then the waters flowed back on their oppressors;
there wasn't a single survivor.
MSG.12:
Then they believed his words were true
and broke out in songs of praise.
MSG.13:
But it wasn't long before they forgot the whole thing,
wouldn't wait to be told what to do.
MSG.14:
They only cared about pleasing themselves in that desert,
provoked God with their insistent demands.
MSG.15:
He gave them exactly what they asked for—
but along with it they got an empty heart.
MSG.16:
One day in camp some grew jealous of Moses,
also of Aaron, holy priest of God.
MSG.17:
The ground opened and swallowed Dathan,
then buried Abiram's gang.
MSG.18:
Fire flared against that rebel crew
and torched them to a cinder.
MSG.19:
They cast in metal a bull calf at Horeb
and worshiped the statue they'd made.
MSG.20:
They traded the Glory
for a cheap piece of sculpture—a grass-chewing bull!
MSG.21:
They forgot God, their very own Savior,
who turned things around in Egypt,
MSG.22:
Who created a world of wonders in the Land of Ham,
who gave that stunning performance at the Red Sea.
MSG.23:
Fed up, God decided to get rid of them—
and except for Moses, his chosen, he would have.
But Moses stood in the gap and deflected God's anger,
prevented it from destroying them utterly.
MSG.24:
They went on to reject the Blessed Land,
didn't believe a word of what God promised.
MSG.25:
They found fault with the life they had
and turned a deaf ear to God's voice.
MSG.26:
Exasperated, God swore
that he'd lay them low in the desert,
MSG.27:
Scattering their children hither and yon,
strewing them all over the earth.
MSG.28:
Then they linked up with Baal Peor,
attending funeral banquets and eating idol food.
MSG.29:
That made God so angry
that a plague spread through their ranks;
MSG.30:
Phinehas stood up and pled their case
and the plague was stopped.
MSG.31:
This was counted to his credit;
his descendants will never forget it.
MSG.32:
They angered God again at Meribah Springs;
this time Moses got mixed up in their evil;
MSG.33:
Because they defied God yet again,
Moses exploded and lost his temper.
MSG.34:
They didn't wipe out those godless cultures
as ordered by God;
MSG.35:
Instead they intermarried with the heathen,
and in time became just like them.
MSG.36:
They worshiped their idols,
were caught in the trap of idols.
MSG.37:
They sacrificed their sons and daughters
at the altars of demon gods.
MSG.38:
They slit the throats of their babies,
murdered their infant girls and boys.
They offered their babies to Canaan's gods;
the blood of their babies stained the land.
MSG.39:
Their way of life stank to high heaven;
they lived like whores.
MSG.40:
And God was furious—a wildfire anger;
he couldn't stand even to look at his people.
MSG.41:
He turned them over to the heathen
so that the people who hated them ruled them.
MSG.42:
Their enemies made life hard for them;
they were tyrannized under that rule.
MSG.43:
Over and over God rescued them, but they never learned—
until finally their sins destroyed them.
MSG.44:
Still, when God saw the trouble they were in
and heard their cries for help,
MSG.45:
He remembered his Covenant with them,
and, immense with love, took them by the hand.
MSG.46:
He poured out his mercy on them
while their captors looked on, amazed.
MSG.47:
Save us, God, our God!
Gather us back out of exile
So we can give thanks to your holy name
and join in the glory when you are praised!
MSG.48:
Blessed be God, Israel's God!
Bless now, bless always!
Oh! Let everyone say Amen!
Hallelujah!

NIV.1:
Psalm 106
Praise the Lord.
Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good;
his love endures forever.
NIV.2:
Who can proclaim the mighty acts of the Lord
or fully declare his praise?
NIV.3:
Blessed are they who maintain justice,
who constantly do what is right.
NIV.4:
Remember me, O Lord, when you show favor to your people,
come to my aid when you save them,
NIV.5:
that I may enjoy the prosperity of your chosen ones,
that I may share in the joy of your nation
and join your inheritance in giving praise.
NIV.6:
We have sinned, even as our fathers did;
we have done wrong and acted wickedly.
NIV.7:
When our fathers were in Egypt,
they gave no thought to your miracles;
they did not remember your many kindnesses,
and they rebelled by the sea, the Red Sea.
NIV.8:
Yet he saved them for his name’s sake,
to make his mighty power known.
NIV.9:
He rebuked the Red Sea, and it dried up;
he led them through the depths as through a desert.
NIV.10:
He saved them from the hand of the foe;
from the hand of the enemy he redeemed them.
NIV.11:
The waters covered their adversaries;
not one of them survived.
NIV.12:
Then they believed his promises
and sang his praise.
NIV.13:
But they soon forgot what he had done
and did not wait for his counsel.
NIV.14:
In the desert they gave in to their craving;
in the wasteland they put God to the test.
NIV.15:
So he gave them what they asked for,
but sent a wasting disease upon them.
NIV.16:
In the camp they grew envious of Moses
and of Aaron, who was consecrated to the Lord.
NIV.17:
The earth opened up and swallowed Dathan;
it buried the company of Abiram.
NIV.18:
Fire blazed among their followers;
a flame consumed the wicked.
NIV.19:
At Horeb they made a calf
and worshiped an idol cast from metal.
NIV.20:
They exchanged their Glory
for an image of a bull, which eats grass.
NIV.21:
They forgot the God who saved them,
who had done great things in Egypt,
NIV.22:
miracles in the land of Ham
and awesome deeds by the Red Sea.
NIV.23:
So he said he would destroy them—
had not Moses, his chosen one,
stood in the breach before him
to keep his wrath from destroying them.
NIV.24:
Then they despised the pleasant land;
they did not believe his promise.
NIV.25:
They grumbled in their tents
and did not obey the Lord.
NIV.26:
So he swore to them with uplifted hand
that he would make them fall in the desert,
NIV.27:
make their descendants fall among the nations
and scatter them throughout the lands.
NIV.28:
They yoked themselves to the Baal of Peor
and ate sacrifices offered to lifeless gods;
NIV.29:
they provoked the Lord to anger by their wicked deeds,
and a plague broke out among them.
NIV.30:
But Phinehas stood up and intervened,
and the plague was checked.
NIV.31:
This was credited to him as righteousness
for endless generations to come.
NIV.32:
By the waters of Meribah they angered the Lord,
and trouble came to Moses because of them;
NIV.33:
for they rebelled against the Spirit of God,
and rash words came from Moses’ lips.
NIV.34:
They did not destroy the peoples
as the Lord had commanded them,
NIV.35:
but they mingled with the nations
and adopted their customs.
NIV.36:
They worshiped their idols,
which became a snare to them.
NIV.37:
They sacrificed their sons
and their daughters to demons.
NIV.38:
They shed innocent blood,
the blood of their sons and daughters,
whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan,
and the land was desecrated by their blood.
NIV.39:
They defiled themselves by what they did;
by their deeds they prostituted themselves.
NIV.40:
Therefore the Lord was angry with his people
and abhorred his inheritance.
NIV.41:
He handed them over to the nations,
and their foes ruled over them.
NIV.42:
Their enemies oppressed them
and subjected them to their power.
NIV.43:
Many times he delivered them,
but they were bent on rebellion
and they wasted away in their sin.
NIV.44:
But he took note of their distress
when he heard their cry;
NIV.45:
for their sake he remembered his covenant
and out of his great love he relented.
NIV.46:
He caused them to be pitied
by all who held them captive.
NIV.47:
Save us, O Lord our God,
and gather us from the nations,
that we may give thanks to your holy name
and glory in your praise.
NIV.48:
Praise be to the Lord, the God of Israel,
from everlasting to everlasting.
Let all the people say, “Amen!”
Praise the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
Praise the LORD!
O give thanks to the LORD, for he is good;
for his steadfast love endures forever.
NRSVCE.2:
Who can utter the mighty doings of the LORD,
or declare all his praise?
NRSVCE.3:
Happy are those who observe justice,
who do righteousness at all times.
NRSVCE.4:
Remember me, O LORD, when you show favor to your people;
help me when you deliver them;
NRSVCE.5:
that I may see the prosperity of your chosen ones,
that I may rejoice in the gladness of your nation,
that I may glory in your heritage.
NRSVCE.6:
Both we and our ancestors have sinned;
we have committed iniquity, have done wickedly.
NRSVCE.7:
Our ancestors, when they were in Egypt,
did not consider your wonderful works;
they did not remember the abundance of your steadfast love,
but rebelled against the Most High at the Red Sea.
NRSVCE.8:
Yet he saved them for his name’s sake,
so that he might make known his mighty power.
NRSVCE.9:
He rebuked the Red Sea, and it became dry;
he led them through the deep as through a desert.
NRSVCE.10:
So he saved them from the hand of the foe,
and delivered them from the hand of the enemy.
NRSVCE.11:
The waters covered their adversaries;
not one of them was left.
NRSVCE.12:
Then they believed his words;
they sang his praise.
NRSVCE.13:
But they soon forgot his works;
they did not wait for his counsel.
NRSVCE.14:
But they had a wanton craving in the wilderness,
and put God to the test in the desert;
NRSVCE.15:
he gave them what they asked,
but sent a wasting disease among them.
NRSVCE.16:
They were jealous of Moses in the camp,
and of Aaron, the holy one of the LORD.
NRSVCE.17:
The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan,
and covered the faction of Abiram.
NRSVCE.18:
Fire also broke out in their company;
the flame burned up the wicked.
NRSVCE.19:
They made a calf at Horeb
and worshiped a cast image.
NRSVCE.20:
They exchanged the glory of God
for the image of an ox that eats grass.
NRSVCE.21:
They forgot God, their Savior,
who had done great things in Egypt,
NRSVCE.22:
wondrous works in the land of Ham,
and awesome deeds by the Red Sea.
NRSVCE.23:
Therefore he said he would destroy them —
had not Moses, his chosen one,
stood in the breach before him,
to turn away his wrath from destroying them.
NRSVCE.24:
Then they despised the pleasant land,
having no faith in his promise.
NRSVCE.25:
They grumbled in their tents,
and did not obey the voice of the LORD.
NRSVCE.26:
Therefore he raised his hand and swore to them
that he would make them fall in the wilderness,
NRSVCE.27:
and would disperse their descendants among the nations,
scattering them over the lands.
NRSVCE.28:
Then they attached themselves to the Baal of Peor,
and ate sacrifices offered to the dead;
NRSVCE.29:
they provoked the LORD to anger with their deeds,
and a plague broke out among them.
NRSVCE.30:
Then Phinehas stood up and interceded,
and the plague was stopped.
NRSVCE.31:
And that has been reckoned to him as righteousness
from generation to generation forever.
NRSVCE.32:
They angered the LORD at the waters of Meribah,
and it went ill with Moses on their account;
NRSVCE.33:
for they made his spirit bitter,
and he spoke words that were rash.
NRSVCE.34:
They did not destroy the peoples,
as the LORD commanded them,
NRSVCE.35:
but they mingled with the nations
and learned to do as they did.
NRSVCE.36:
They served their idols,
which became a snare to them.
NRSVCE.37:
They sacrificed their sons
and their daughters to the demons;
NRSVCE.38:
they poured out innocent blood,
the blood of their sons and daughters,
whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan;
and the land was polluted with blood.
NRSVCE.39:
Thus they became unclean by their acts,
and prostituted themselves in their doings.
NRSVCE.40:
Then the anger of the LORD was kindled against his people,
and he abhorred his heritage;
NRSVCE.41:
he gave them into the hand of the nations,
so that those who hated them ruled over them.
NRSVCE.42:
Their enemies oppressed them,
and they were brought into subjection under their power.
NRSVCE.43:
Many times he delivered them,
but they were rebellious in their purposes,
and were brought low through their iniquity.
NRSVCE.44:
Nevertheless he regarded their distress
when he heard their cry.
NRSVCE.45:
For their sake he remembered his covenant,
and showed compassion according to the abundance of his steadfast love.
NRSVCE.46:
He caused them to be pitied
by all who held them captive.
NRSVCE.47:
Save us, O LORD our God,
and gather us from among the nations,
that we may give thanks to your holy name
and glory in your praise.
NRSVCE.48:
Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel,
from everlasting to everlasting.
And let all the people say, “Amen.”
Praise the LORD!

NET.1: Praise the LORD! Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, and his loyal love endures!
NET.2: Who can adequately recount the LORD’s mighty acts, or relate all his praiseworthy deeds?
NET.3: How blessed are those who promote justice, and do what is right all the time!
NET.4: Remember me, O LORD, when you show favor to your people! Pay attention to me, when you deliver,
NET.5: so I may see the prosperity of your chosen ones, rejoice along with your nation, and boast along with the people who belong to you.
NET.6: We have sinned like our ancestors; we have done wrong, we have done evil.
NET.7: Our ancestors in Egypt failed to appreciate your miraculous deeds, they failed to remember your many acts of loyal love, and they rebelled at the sea, by the Red Sea.
NET.8: Yet he delivered them for the sake of his reputation, that he might reveal his power.
NET.9: He shouted at the Red Sea and it dried up; he led them through the deep water as if it were a desert.
NET.10: He delivered them from the power of the one who hated them, and rescued them from the power of the enemy.
NET.11: The water covered their enemies; not even one of them survived.
NET.12: They believed his promises; they sang praises to him.
NET.13: They quickly forgot what he had done; they did not wait for his instructions.
NET.14: In the wilderness they had an insatiable craving for meat; they challenged God in the desert.
NET.15: He granted their request, then struck them with a disease.
NET.16: In the camp they resented Moses, and Aaron, the LORD’s holy priest.
NET.17: The earth opened up and swallowed Dathan; it engulfed the group led by Abiram.
NET.18: Fire burned their group; the flames scorched the wicked.
NET.19: They made an image of a calf at Horeb, and worshiped a metal idol.
NET.20: They traded their majestic God for the image of an ox that eats grass.
NET.21: They rejected the God who delivered them, the one who performed great deeds in Egypt,
NET.22: amazing feats in the land of Ham, mighty acts by the Red Sea.
NET.23: He threatened to destroy them, but Moses, his chosen one, interceded with him and turned back his destructive anger.
NET.24: They rejected the fruitful land; they did not believe his promise.
NET.25: They grumbled in their tents; they did not obey the LORD.
NET.26: So he made a solemn vow that he would make them die in the desert,
NET.27: make their descendants die among the nations, and scatter them among foreign lands.
NET.28: They worshiped Baal of Peor, and ate sacrifices offered to the dead.
NET.29: They made the LORD angry by their actions, and a plague broke out among them.
NET.30: Phinehas took a stand and intervened, and the plague subsided.
NET.31: This brought him a reward, an eternal gift.
NET.32: They made him angry by the waters of Meribah, and Moses suffered because of them,
NET.33: for they aroused his temper, and he spoke rashly.
NET.34: They did not destroy the nations, as the LORD had commanded them to do.
NET.35: They mixed in with the nations and learned their ways.
NET.36: They worshiped their idols, which became a snare to them.
NET.37: They sacrificed their sons and daughters to demons.
NET.38: They shed innocent blood — the blood of their sons and daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan. The land was polluted by bloodshed.
NET.39: They were defiled by their deeds, and unfaithful in their actions.
NET.40: So the LORD was angry with his people and despised the people who belong to him.
NET.41: He handed them over to the nations, and those who hated them ruled over them.
NET.42: Their enemies oppressed them; they were subject to their authority.
NET.43: Many times he delivered them, but they had a rebellious attitude, and degraded themselves by their sin.
NET.44: Yet he took notice of their distress, when he heard their cry for help.
NET.45: He remembered his covenant with them, and relented because of his great loyal love.
NET.46: He caused all their conquerors to have pity on them.
NET.47: Deliver us, O LORD, our God! Gather us from among the nations! Then we will give thanks to your holy name, and boast about your praiseworthy deeds.
NET.48: The LORD God of Israel deserves praise, in the future and forevermore. Let all the people say, “We agree! Praise the LORD!”
